reforms.description: The Japanese government has implemented various measures to enhance cooperation between universities and industry, including a fund of 45 billion yen to support this collaboration. Additionally, the Growth-Supporting Funding Facility was introduced to encourage banks to lend to firms in growth industries, while measures to promote industrial competitiveness and innovation are being implemented to stimulate private investment in R&D and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance collaboration and funding for innovation, which is expected to raise productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect to support innovation and collaboration between universities and industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at enhancing cooperation and funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced Special Zones for Reconstruction in 2011... can receive special treatment, notably: i) land-use restructuring facilitation; ii) relaxation of regulations and administrative procedures; iii) financial support; and iv) tax reduction.

reforms.description: Public investment in programs to create leading universities increased significantly, from 12 billion yen in FY2011 to 21 billion yen in FY2012, aiming to enhance the quality of higher education and research output.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for leading universities is expected to improve research quality and output, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase has been executed to enhance university capabilities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in funding represents a significant investment in higher education and research capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: Public investment in programs to create leading universities increased from 12 billion yen in FY2011 to 21 billion yen in FY2012.

reforms.description: Japan launched a feed-in-tariff (FIT) programme in 2012, obligating general electric utilities to purchase electricity from renewable energy producers at a fixed long-term price, thereby promoting the development of renewable energy sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The FIT programme is designed to stimulate investment in renewable energy, which can enhance energy security and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The FIT programme has been successfully launched and is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The FIT programme represents a major structural change in Japan's energy policy aimed at promoting renewable energy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2012, Japan launched a feed-in-tariff (FIT) programme, which obliges the general electric utilities to purchase electricity from almost all renewable energy producers to promote renewable energy.
